subject: Get resources filenames from globals, not hostvars
commit_message:
Get resources filenames from globals, not hostvars

As resources_dir, resources_filename and aux_resources_filename vars
are defined for all hosts globally (group_vars/all.yml or
application/application_configuration.yml) there is no use in calling
them by hostvars.

Change-Id: Iab1ff448aedb1ae52db40f8685d05a0967f1a52f
Issue-ID: OOM-1632
Signed-off-by:  Bartek Grzybowski <b.grzybowski@partner.samsung.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

I'm working on bigger re-factoring of this role and this part addressed by this commit was one of the first things I did.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> I'm working on bigger re-factoring of this role and this part addressed by this commit was one of the first things I did.

Then i guess you'll be getting rid of those eg. "{{ hostvars[groups.resources.0].ansible_host }}" host references via indices for referring them by name, like "{{ hostvars['resource_host'].ansible_host }}", right? Because that's something i was also about to refactor.

Patch Set 1:

> > Patch Set 1:
 > >
 > > I'm working on bigger re-factoring of this role and this part
 > addressed by this commit was one of the first things I did.
 > 
 > Then i guess you'll be getting rid of those eg. "{{
 > hostvars[groups.resources.0].ansible_host }}" host references via
 > indices for referring them by name, like "{{ hostvars['resource_host'].ansible_host
 > }}", right? Because that's something i was also about to refactor.

Actually no because that name is host alias and can be any text changed by user, so cannot put that to the code.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

If it works then great, looks better
